messages.content:
PUT answer in [answer].

A: What’s her postcode?
B: I’m fairly c[answer] it’s BC2 6GH.
I'm fairly certain it's BC2 6GH.

A TASTE OF YOUR LIFE
In the BBC chat show series A Taste of My Life, TV chef Nigel Slater talks to celebrities about their strongest food memories and particular dishes which have shaped their lives. Comic actor Sanjeev Bhaskar reminisces about the contrast between bland ‘meat and two veg’ school dinners and the Indian food he was served at home. Fellow chef Nigella Lawson tells her life story through food. Do you have a memory of a favourite dish associated with a special event? Can you remember who cooked it? We asked for your best food memories.

It has to be boiled eggs with ‘soldiers' My mum used to give this to us as kids. The egg should be soft-boiled so the yolk is still runny, and the toast is spread with melted butter and cut into thin strips (tall and straight, like soldiers), which you can hold and dip into the top of the egg. Now I give it to my kids and they love it. It's healthy and I hope it will give them the same happy memories.	Claire, UK

My grandmother’s homemade pasta. She used to make pasta on the kitchen table every day. I can remember her laying out the pasta in the morning when I got up for breakfast. Nowadays, people have pasta machines, but she would roll out the pasta by hand, and when we came home from school, there was always a plate of fresh tagliatelle waiting for us. The dry pasta you buy in the shops just doesn't taste the same.	Mariella, Italy

Tortilla. Whenever I feel like I'm missing my home country, I cook a tortilla. Its a kind of omelette made with potato and onion and you can find it in all the tapas bars in Spain. But, of course, the one I make at home is the best. I have so many happy memories of eating tortilla. They go all through my life, from when I was a child, a student at university, eating with friends. Tortilla is everywhere. I couldn’t live without it	Jorge, Spain
When I go back to Penang (Malaysia), I go straight to a hawker (street seller) and order a bowl of Penang Hokkien Mee (prawn noodle soup).

It’s one dish I always miss. There is something about the combination of the prawns and the chilli in the soup with the noodles. I just love it You can make it at home too, but it tastes better when you eat it out on the street. There’s an atmosphere, with the heat, and the noise of the traffic and people shouting. It’s more than just a dish - rt's an experience. And I always look forward to it Penang’s food is part of my life. In fact, I don’t think you can find the same taste anywhere else.	Irene, US

Answer the question.

Who likes to eat this dish when they return to a special place?
Irene likes to eat Penang Hokkien Mee (prawn noodle soup) when she returns to Penang, Malaysia.

Choose the correct variant to complete the definition.

boiled: cooked in (water / oil)
boiled: cooked in water.

Choose the correct variant to complete the definition.

reminisces: talk or think about (pleasant / terrible) events in your past
reminisces: talk or think about pleasant events in your past.

Write the reported speech sentences in direct speech.

They said they couldn’t find their tickets for the concert the next day.
"We can't find our tickets for the concert tomorrow," they said.

Write the reported speech sentences in direct speech.

Daisy said she’d see us the next day.
"I'll see you tomorrow," Daisy said.

Write the direct speech sentences in reported speech.

‘You mustn’t use the internet for longer than an hour a day, Tom’, said his father.
Tom's father told him, "You mustn't use the internet for longer than an hour a day."

Write the direct speech sentences in reported speech.

‘I might be late coming back, Liz’, said Jen.
Jen told Liz that she "might be late coming back."

Write the direct speech sentences in reported speech.

‘Have you met each other before?’, asked Li.
Li asked, "Have you met each other before?"

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

I explained that the food (taste) bad.
I explained that the food tasted bad.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

She promised that she (meet) us outside the cinema at 7.30
She promised that she would meet us outside the cinema at 7.30.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

She offered (take) us sightseeing.
She offered to take us sightseeing.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

I suggested that we (leave) early.
I suggested that we leave early.

What type of cybercrime is it?

making one computer look like another in order to gain unauthorized access
This is a case of cybercrime related to Spoofing.

What type of cybercrime is it?

the illegal copy and distribution of copyrighted software, games or music files
This is a case of cybercrime related to Software Piracy.

Complete the sentence with one word.

A [answer] protects a company intranet from outside attacks.
A firewall protects a company intranet from outside attacks.

Complete the sentence with one word.

You can download [answer] from the Net; this type of software is available free of charge but protected by copyright.
You can download freeware from the Net; this type of software is available free of charge but protected by copyright.

Complete the sentence with one word.

Users have to enter a [answer] to gain access to a network
Users have to enter a password to gain access to a network.

Rearrange the letters to complete the sentences to do with the environment.

These days, more people are choosing to buy (cirnaog) food because the methods of production are better for the environment.
These days, more people are choosing to buy organic food because the methods of production are better for the environment.

Rearrange the letters to complete the sentences to do with the environment.

Since electronic books have become available, it’s become even easier to buy (densdnohca) books at really low prices.
Since electronic books have become available, it's become even easier to buy secondhand books at really low prices.

Rearrange the letters to complete the sentences to do with the environment.

Our family never buys much (drepsoces) food as we prefer to eat healthily and cook fresh food.
Our family never buys much processed food as we prefer to eat healthily and cook fresh food.

Complete the words with the correct prefix.

I wish you’d stop [answer] cooking the meat. It makes it very tough to eat
I wish you'd stop overcooking the meat. It make it very tough to eat.

Complete the words with the correct prefix.

We completely [answer]estimated how long it would take to get to Strasbourg and arrived really late for the wedding.
We completely underestimated how long it would take to get to Strasbourg and arrived really late for the wedding.

Complete the words with the correct prefix.

It’s easy to [answer]pronounce words in a new language, so don’t worry if I correct you.
It's easy to mispronounce words in a new language, so don't worry if I correct you.

Complete the words with the correct prefix.

The judge ruled that the company had used [answer]ethical and illegal business practices.
The judge ruled that the company had used unethical and illegal business practices.

Complete the words with the correct prefix.

Ali likes [answer]using objects where possible and has just made a bed cover from old clothes.
Ali likes reusing objects where possible and has just made a bed cover from old clothes.

Complete the sentence.

The airport information board said that it would take fifteen minutes to walk from the duty-free shop to  [blank] 23.

[blank]: passports / boards / hand / priority / check / borading / aisle / gate
The airport information board said that it would take fifteen minutes to walk from the duty-free shop to Gate 23.

Complete the sentence.

We were asked to have our [blank] cards ready and they were inspected before we were allowed to proceed onto the plane.

[blank]: passports / boards / hand / priority / check / borading / aisle / gate
We were asked to have our boarding cards ready and they were inspected before we were allowed to proceed onto the plane.

Complete the sentence.

I need to [blank] in at least two hours before my flight so could you pick me up at 10 o’clock?

[blank]: passports / boards / hand / priority / check / borading / aisle / gate
I need to check in at least two hours before my flight so could you pick me up at 10 o'clock?

Match the sentence with a suitable reporting verb.

Let’s go to see that exhibition. It’s got great reviews. ( explain / refuse / suggest / offer / invite / warn )
suggest
